chunks.text: The " rule of law " is the principle that all people are equal before the law and that no power holder may stand above the law.
This principle, which began in ancient Rome, forms the foundation of modern democratic society.
A constitution stands as the supreme law, and every other law is placed beneath it.
there are many countries where the rule of law does not work well enough.
Corruption spreads, and those in power sometimes twist the law to their own advantage.
Judges may come under political pressure and lose the ability to make independent decisions.
A person who commits a crime is brought to trial, and if found guilty
the purpose of punishment has been shifting from mere penalty toward rehabilitation.
The aim is to help former prisoners return to society and to prevent them from falling back into criminal conduct.
Citizens, too, carry the responsibility of taking part in the enforcement of law.
When civilian raise their voices against wrongdoing and demand transparency of the system
they support the health of democratic society.
Law is the promise of a society.
The will to keep that promise, and to reform it as needed
is what builds the foundation of a free and just society.
